meaning: n.佩科里诺干酪,羊乳干酪
examples: Try it together with a cold cut platter, and wedges of Pecorino.
So soft, the Pecorino begins to "cry" (as the locals put it) as the digested fatty liquid leaks out.
On the isle of Sardinia in Italy, they prefer Pecorino. They also like it served a very specific way.
Our saffron gnocchi was plump and plaint dressed up with garlic, artichoke and shaved pecorino.
Finished with a showering of fresh basil and Pecorino cheese, the pasta was, and still is, perfection.
